items.title: Some of my favorite things in Opus 4.7: - Very good at async work and following instructions -…
items.content: Some of my favorite things in Opus 4.7: - Very good at async work and following instructions - Effort levels are far more predictable for token control (+ new xhigh level) - No more downscaling of high-res images - Noticeably more taste in UIs, slides, docs https://t.co/GlTiMfwFVa

items.title: New version of Claude Code in the desktop app dropped today with tons of new features and perfo…
items.content: New version of Claude Code in the desktop app dropped today with tons of new features and performance improvements. Between Cowork and Code, I've found I don't really need to open other apps (or even my terminal) to do most of my work now. https://t.co/0HuRKunqzx

items.title: Allowing Sonnet to "phone a friend" (i.e. call Opus) increases performance while also reducing…
items.content: Allowing Sonnet to "phone a friend" (i.e. call Opus) increases performance while also reducing total cost since it reduces tokens spent trying to solve more complex tasks https://t.co/UmsWK4Xn32 https://t.co/aeoH2E0kb1

items.title: I've found Managed Agents to somehow be both the fastest way to hack together a weekend agent p…
items.content: I've found Managed Agents to somehow be both the fastest way to hack together a weekend agent project and the most robust way to ship one to millions of users. It eliminates all the complexity of self-hosting an agent but still allows a great degree of flexibility with setting up your harness, tools, skills, etc.
